entries.content:
Achieving seamless coordination between AI agents and humans is crucial for
real-world applications, yet it remains a significant open challenge. Hanabi is
a cooperative card game featuring imperfect information, constrained
communication, theory of mind requirements, and coordinated action -- making it
an ideal testbed for human-AI coordination. We present baseline results for both two- and
three- player Hanabi scenarios. com/FLAIROx/ah2ac2}

entries.topic:
Managing level of detail through head-tracked peripheral degradation: a
  model and resulting design principles
entries.content:
Previous work has demonstrated the utility of reductions in the level of
detail (LOD) in the periphery of head-tracked, large field of view displays. This paper provides a psychophysically based model, centered around an eye/head
movement tradeoff, that explains the effectiveness of peripheral degradation
and suggests how peripherally degraded displays should be designed. Inset area, however, was significant: performance with displays
subtending at least 30 degrees of horizontal and vertical ang...

entries.topic:
An evaluation of level of detail degradation in head-mounted display
  peripheries
entries.content:
A paradigm for the design of systems that manage level of detail in virtual
environments is proposed. As an example of the prototyping step in this
paradigm, a user study was performed to evaluate the effectiveness of high
detail insets used with head-mounted displays. Primary dependent measures were search time on trials with correct
identification, and the percentage of all trials correctly identified. Further research is being performed to examine the effect of
varying task complexity, inset ...

entries.topic:
Lightweight Fingernail Haptic Device: Unobstructed Fingerpad Force and
  Vibration Feedback for Enhanced Virtual Dexterous Manipulation
entries.content:
This study presents a lightweight, wearable fingertip haptic device that
provides physics-based haptic feedback for dexterous manipulation in virtual
environments without hindering real-world interactions. The device, designed
with thin strings and actuators attached to the fingernails, ensures minimal
weight (1. Our results show that participants could
perceive and respond to pressure and vibration feedback. This research offers a potential solution for designing haptic
interfaces that balance ...

entries.topic:
AGTCNet: A Graph-Temporal Approach for Principled Motor Imagery EEG
  Classification
entries.content:
Brain-computer interface (BCI) technology utilizing electroencephalography
(EEG) marks a transformative innovation, empowering motor-impaired individuals
to engage with their environment on equal footing. Despite its promising
potential, developing subject-invariant and session-invariant BCI systems
remains a significant challenge due to the inherent complexity and variability
of neural activity across individuals and over time, compounded by EEG hardware
constraints. 65% faster inference
time, ...

entries.topic: A Systematic Review of Human-AI Co-Creativity
entries.content:
The co creativity community is making significant progress in developing more
sophisticated and tailored systems to support and enhance human creativity. Design considerations from prior work can serve as a valuable and efficient
foundation for future systems. Our findings suggest that systems offering high user control lead to
greater satisfaction, trust, and a stronger sense of ownership over creative
outcomes. Despite recent advancements, important gaps remain, such as limited support for
ear...

entries.topic:
"Who Should I Believe?": User Interpretation and Decision-Making When a
  Family Healthcare Robot Contradicts Human Memory
entries.content:
Advancements in robotic capabilities for providing physical assistance,
psychological support, and daily health management are making the deployment of
intelligent healthcare robots in home environments increasingly feasible in the
near future. However, challenges arise when the information provided by these
robots contradicts users' memory, raising concerns about user trust and
decision-making. Results indicate that robot transparency influenced
users' interpretation of information discrepancie...
